parsed_candidate.joint_topic: Genome-Inspired Modular Routing for Mixture-of-Experts Body Plans
parsed_candidate.thesis: A single MoE network can be trained so its router acts like a developmental regulatory gene network, selectively switching on combinations of expert modules to induce many distinct functional 'body plans' (task-specialized subnetworks) from one shared parameter genome.
parsed_candidate.mechanism: The router regulates which expert modules activate per token, and we treat these activation patterns as developmental switches: distinct module-activation combinations induce distinct specialized subnetworks, so one shared parameter set produces many task-specific architectures depending on which modules switch on.
parsed_candidate.primary_quote: a single genome induces many distinct body plans depending on which modules switch on

verification_plan.query: mixture-of-experts routing modular specialization emergent subnetworks
developmental gene regulatory networks modularity analogy neural network architecture
conditional computation expert activation patterns task specialization MoE
developmental biology body plan modular neural network weight sharing
regulatory gene networks developmental modules body plan induction
sparse mixture of experts router gating expert subnetwork specialization

prompt:
You are merging two research "atoms" into ONE candidate paradigm-shift research niche in AI / LLMs.

ATOM A (id ML_E1_A01, source arxiv): "Sparse mixture-of-experts routing activates only a few expert subnetworks per token, so the router gates compute toward the inputs each expert specializes in."
ATOM B (id BIO_E1_A02, source essay): "In developmental biology, regulatory gene networks selectively activate developmental modules, so a single genome induces many distinct body plans depending on which modules switch on."

Combine the mechanism in ATOM A with the mechanism/principle in ATOM B into a single, concrete, testable research direction.

Output ONLY a fenced ```json block (no prose before or after) with EXACTLY these fields:
{
  "joint_topic": "<=12 word title of the merged niche",
  "thesis": "1-2 sentence concrete research thesis",
  "mechanism": "1-2 sentences describing the causal mechanism; MUST use concrete causal verbs such as induces, produces, causes, transforms, regulates, activates, inhibits, routes",
  "operator": "a short relation label such as INDUCES or TRANSFORMS; do NOT use ANALOGY_TRANSFERS_TO_OPEN",
  "primary_quote": "a quote of at least 30 characters copied EXACTLY (verbatim, character-for-character) from ATOM A's text or ATOM B's text; do not paraphrase or invent",
  "verification_plan": [{"source": "<name>", "query": "<search query>"}]
}

Rules:
- primary_quote MUST be an exact substring of one atom's text. Copy it; do not invent.
- verification_plan MUST list at least 5 entries with 5 DISTINCT source names.
- Output nothing except the json block.
